items.title: The Oil Shock Isn’t Over
items.description: Releasing barrels of crude was the easy part.

items.title: US Immigration Policies Hammer Mexican Grocery Chain Chedraui
items.description: President Donald Trump’s immigration crackdown is causing the worst rout on the Mexican stock exchange this year.

items.title: DoubleLine Says Higher Bond Yields to Help Fed Keep Rates Steady
items.description: DoubleLine is positioning in shorter-dated government bonds on the view that Kevin Warsh’s credibility with investors will help allow Federal Reserve officials to keep interest rates steady this year.

items.title: Angola Signs $900 Million Port Deals With Chinese Firms
items.description: Angola signed deals with Chinese investors to develop the Barra do Dande Integrated Development Free Zone outside Luanda, as the government seeks to establish a regional logistics hub.

items.title: Gilts Rattled by New UK PM’s Comment on Fiscal Flexibility

items.title: KKR, Vauban Among Final Contenders to Buy Portugal’s DStelecom
items.description: KKR & Co. and Vauban Infrastructure Partners are competing to acquire a majority stake in Portuguese fiber operator DStelecom, according to people familiar with the matter.

items.title: Netflix Taps US High-Grade Bond Market With $1 Billion Debt Sale
items.description: Netflix Inc. is tapping the US high-grade bond market for the first time since its debut deal two years ago, as slowing sales growth fuels investor scrutiny of the company’s future.

items.title: Lionheart (CUB) Nears $400 Million Deal for Venezuela Oil Stake

items.title: BlackRock Eyes More Than $12 Billion Debt for Data Center
items.description: BlackRock Inc. is looking to sell more than $12 billion of bonds to help finance a Meta Platforms Inc. data center campus in El Paso, Texas, according to people with knowledge of the transaction, the

items.title: Goldman Sees Turkey Likely Tolerating Faster Lira Slide
items.description: Goldman Sachs economists expect Turkey to allow a more rapid depreciation of the lira in order to prioritize external-balance stabilization over disinflation.

items.title: US Stocks Rebound From Selloff as Nvidia, Other Chipmakers Rise
items.description: US stocks advanced on Monday, paring last week’s slump, as chipmakers rebounded ahead of a deluge of earnings reports that will test whether Big Tech’s profits can match high expectations.

items.title: Alkegen Plans Prepackaged Bankruptcy in Deal for Fresh Cash
items.description: Alkegen has signed a restructuring deal with lenders that will inject the specialty materials maker with new money and slash its debt load by about $3.1 billion.

items.title: Latin America Outperforms as Emerging Market Currencies Gain
items.description: Emerging-market currencies gained to start the week, with Latin American names getting a boost from a drop in volatility and carry appeal.

items.title: Stocks Get Lift From Chipmakers as Oil Rally Wanes: Markets Wrap
items.description: Stocks rose, halting back-to-back losses, as chipmakers bounced while a rally in oil faded on hopes the US and Iran could revive their peace deal.
